name: August
description: Augustus Caesar's namesake month — Perseids, Hiroshima, MLK's dream, and the dog days of summer.

questions.q: How many days does August have?
Which Roman emperor is August named after?
What was August originally called in the Roman calendar?

source.notes: Latin for 'sixth' — when the year began in March.

questions.q: What is August's traditional birthstone?

source.notes: Sardonyx and spinel are also recognized.

questions.q: Which annual meteor shower peaks in mid-August, often called 'the tears of St. Lawrence'?

source.notes: Peaks around August 11–13. Reliably one of the best meteor showers each year.

questions.q: On what date in 1945 did the United States drop the atomic bomb on Hiroshima?

source.notes: August 6, 1945; Nagasaki was bombed three days later on August 9.

questions.q: On what date in 1963 did Martin Luther King Jr. deliver his 'I Have a Dream' speech at the Lincoln Memorial?

source.notes: March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom; estimated crowd of 250,000.

questions.q: On which August date did Richard Nixon resign the U.S. presidency?
On what August date in 1997 did Princess Diana die in a car crash in Paris?

source.notes: Crash in the Pont de l'Alma tunnel.

questions.q: What major event began on August 1, 1914 — drawing Britain into the conflict?

source.notes: Germany declared war on Russia on Aug 1; Britain declared war on Germany on Aug 4.

questions.q: On what August date in 1947 did India gain independence from British rule?

source.notes: August 15, 1947. Pakistan's Independence Day is the day before, August 14.

questions.q: Which volcano erupted in August 79 CE, burying Pompeii and Herculaneum?

source.notes: Likely erupted on October 24 per recent scholarship — but classical date was August 24.

questions.q: Which Asian country celebrates its National Day on August 9?

source.notes: Marks Singapore's separation from Malaysia in 1965.

questions.q: On what August date does Switzerland celebrate its national day?

source.notes: Celebrates the founding of the Swiss Confederation in 1291.

questions.q: Which two zodiac signs fall in August?

source.notes: Leo runs Aug 22; Virgo begins Aug 23.

questions.q: Which 1969 music festival was held August 15–18, in upstate New York?

source.notes: Held on Max Yasgur's farm in Bethel, NY.

questions.q: On what August date in 1977 did Elvis Presley die at Graceland?
On August 8, 1969, the Beatles famously crossed which London street for their iconic album cover photo?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: original Q falsely claimed an August 1969 'Lonely Hearts Club Band' album; replaced with the August 8, 1969 zebra-crossing photo fact.

questions.q: What is the popular nickname for the hottest stretch of summer days in late July and August?

source.notes: Named for the heliacal rising of the 'dog star' Sirius.

questions.q: Which country observes Independence Day on August 17, after declaring independence from the Netherlands in 1945?
